level_signals: 1-3+ years of experience in revenue operations, sales strategy, business operations, sales finance, or similar
Moderate oversight expected ('run with projects autonomously with moderate oversight')
Compensation range $98,649 - $109,610 consistent with junior-to-mid IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 1-3+ years of experience in revenue operations, sales strategy, business operations, sales finance, or similar; preferably at a Fintech or SaaS company
must_haves.qualifier: preferably at a Fintech or SaaS company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Analytical mindset with a desire for high data precision and accuracy
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Comfortable working cross-functionally and managing requests from multiple stakeholders simultaneously
Self-motivated and able to run with projects autonomously with moderate oversight
Basic to moderate SQL skills with experience in data visualization tools (Looker or Hex experience a plus)
Comfortable working within CRM data models to extract, report on and interpret data (Salesforce preferred)
Meticulous attention to detail, including in documentation

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Exposure to sales compensation methodologies and tracking
Experience supporting revenue annual planning cycles
Knowledge of revenue recognition principles and finance systems
Familiarity with marketing platforms such as Qualified or Leandata, sales engagement platforms like Outreach or Nooks, and sales management platforms such as Atrium or Gong.
Experience leveraging AI for analytics or process optimization
Basic to moderate SQL skills with experience in data visualization tools (Looker or Hex experience a plus)

scope.surface: Revenue Operations — reporting, analytics, pipeline health, capacity and funnel modeling, operational solutions
scope.business_model: B2B fintech SaaS (corporate cards, banking, spend management, travel software)
primary_metrics_inferred: Pipeline health and funnel conversion metrics
core_responsibilities: Create and update dashboards, reports, and presentations to give revenue leaders and operations partners the data they need to run their businesses
Proactively identify opportunities to provide additional reporting or analytical clarity to better understand business performance
Monitor pipeline health and funnel conversion metrics, surfacing trends and anomalies to sales and revenue operations leadership
Run deep dive analyses to identify challenges and opportunities within the business
Partner with Revenue Operations business partners and revenue leaders to support the design, implementation, documentation, and success tracking of operational solutions
Assist with building complex capacity and funnel models for annual planning and scenario evaluation
Respond to ad hoc data and reporting requests from sales leadership and cross-functional partners

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as possible part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear and specific list of responsibilities and required tools
Role sits within a well-defined Revenue Operations function with named cross-functional partners
Equity mentioned as part of total compensation package
Company is an established, well-funded fintech with recognizable customers (DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom)
red_flags: Location type not stated in JD — fully remote, hybrid, or onsite is unclear
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned
The '1-3+ years' phrasing is slightly ambiguous — could imply a wide range of seniority expectations
Looker/Hex listed as a must-have SQL/viz requirement but then also listed again as a nice-to-have — mild duplication/inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and comp)

compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate's location')
ambiguities: Looker and Hex are mentioned within the SQL/data viz requirement as 'a plus' but are also captured as a separate nice-to-have. The parent requirement is treated as hard (SQL is required), while the specific tool experience is soft.

Salesforce is noted as 'preferred' within a requirement otherwise framed as a must-have (CRM comfort is required; Salesforce specifically is preferred). Treated the CRM capability as hard and Salesforce preference as a qualifier.
No location or remote policy specified anywhere in the JD.
